alert_desc: Processes running for all users were queried with ps command.
description: The alert indicates that the 'ps auxwww' command was executed by the 'www-data' user. This command is commonly used for process discovery to list all running processes on the system, which is a technique often employed by attackers for reconnaissance after gaining initial access to a web server.
keywords: ps auxwww, process discovery, system information discovery

original_alert.full_log: Sep 22 18:51:00 videoserver sshd[17243]: Accepted publickey for root from 10.12.0.222 port 40718 ssh2: ED25519 SHA256:erECc93zjoYONyq1nNcu4EwrYe78nzBExDzOmsokkrc

description: The alert indicates a successful SSH authentication for the root user from an external IP address using public key authentication. This behavior relates to the use of remote services and the authentication of valid accounts.
keywords: Remote Services, SSH, Valid Accounts

original_alert.full_log: Sep 22 18:51:00 videoserver sshd[17243]: pam_unix(sshd:session): session opened for user root(uid=0) by (uid=0)

description: The alert indicates a successful SSH login session opened for the root user on the host videoserver. This behavior relates to authentication and access control, specifically the use of privileged accounts via remote access protocols.
keywords: SSH remote login, root user authentication, T1078 Valid Accounts

original_alert.full_log: ossec: Agent stopped: 'videoserver->any'.

description: The Wazuh agent on host videoserver (172.17.100.121) has stopped, which may indicate an attempt to disable security monitoring or evade detection by terminating security services.
keywords: Impair Defenses, Indicator Removal, Service Stop

original_alert.full_log: Sep 22 18:53:21 videoserver sudo:     root : PWD=/usr/share/zoneminder/www ; USER=www-data ; COMMAND=/usr/bin/true

decoder.name: sudo
decoder.ftscomment: First time user executed the sudo command

description: The alert indicates that the root user executed a command as the www-data user via sudo for the first time. This behavior involves the use of sudo to switch user contexts, which is a common technique for privilege escalation or lateral movement within a system.
keywords: sudo, privilege escalation, abuse of sudo

original_alert.full_log: Sep 22 18:53:21 videoserver sudo:     root : PWD=/usr/share/zoneminder/www ; USER=www-data ; COMMAND=/usr/bin/zmdc.pl check

decoder.name: sudo
decoder.ftscomment: First time user executed the sudo command

description: The alert indicates that the root user executed the command /usr/bin/zmdc.pl with the check argument while acting as the www-data user. This involves the use of sudo to execute a specific script within the ZoneMinder directory, which is a common pattern for administrative tasks but can also be associated with privilege escalation or lateral movement if performed by unauthorized accounts.
keywords: sudo, privilege escalation, zmdc.pl, process execution

rule.description: New user added to the system.

original_alert.full_log: Sep 22 18:56:00 videoserver useradd[1658]: new user: name=webadmin, UID=1003, GID=1003, home=/home/webadmin, shell=/bin/bash, from=none

alert_desc: New user added to the system.
description: The system recorded the creation of a new user account named webadmin using the useradd command. This activity is associated with account manipulation and persistence techniques.
keywords: Create Account, Local Account

original_alert.full_log: Sep 22 18:59:00 videoserver sudo: webadmin : PWD=/home/webadmin ; USER=root ; COMMAND=/usr/bin/lspci

decoder.name: sudo
decoder.ftscomment: First time user executed the sudo command

description: The user webadmin successfully executed the command /usr/bin/lspci with root privileges via sudo. This activity involves privilege escalation and the execution of system enumeration tools to gather hardware information.
keywords: sudo, privilege escalation, lspci

alert_desc: Apache: Multiple Invalid URI requests from same source.
description: The alert indicates a series of directory traversal attempts (using ../ sequences) targeting sensitive system files like /etc/passwd, boot.ini, and win.ini. This behavior is characteristic of an automated web scanner or an attacker attempting to perform unauthorized file system access on the web server.
keywords: Path Traversal, Directory Traversal, Web Application Scanning

rule.description: Apache: Attempt to access forbidden directory index.

original_alert.full_log: [Mon Sep 22 18:30:44.603660 2025] [autoindex:error] [pid 661] [client 192.42.1.174:33500] AH01276: Cannot serve directory /var/www/default/log/: No matching DirectoryIndex (index.html,index.cgi,index.pl,index.php,index.xhtml,index.htm) found, and server-generated directory index forbidden by Options directive

alert_desc: Apache: Attempt to access forbidden directory index.
description: An external client attempted to access a directory on an Apache web server that does not have an index file and has directory listing disabled. This behavior is often associated with reconnaissance or directory enumeration to identify sensitive files or server structure.
keywords: Directory Listing, Reconnaissance, Discovery

alert_desc: Executed recursive query of all files using ls command.
description: The alert indicates that a process running as the 'www-data' user executed a recursive file discovery command using 'find' and 'ls' to inspect sensitive system directories, including Java system preferences. This behavior is consistent with reconnaissance activities where an attacker attempts to map the file system or locate configuration files for potential privilege escalation or sensitive information harvesting.
keywords: T1083, T1059.004, File and Directory Discovery, Unix Shell

original_alert.full_log: Dec 15 11:10:45 inetfw kernel: [ 1638.994908] device ens3 entered promiscuous mode

description: The network interface ens3 on the host inetfw has entered promiscuous mode, which allows the device to capture all network traffic passing through the interface. This behavior is often associated with network reconnaissance, traffic interception, or unauthorized packet sniffing.
keywords: network sniffing, promiscuous mode, packet capture
